faqs.question: When should I consider re-piping my home?
faqs.answer: Consider re-piping if your home has galvanized steel pipes, you experience frequent leaks, notice rusty or discolored water, have low water pressure throughout the house, or your home is over 50 years old.

faqs.question: What is the difference between copper and PEX piping?
faqs.answer: Copper is durable and long-lasting but more expensive. PEX is flexible, resistant to corrosion, easier to install, and more affordable. Both are excellent choices depending on your needs and budget.

faqs.question: How long does a whole-house re-pipe take?
faqs.answer: A typical whole-house re-pipe takes 2-5 days depending on the size of your home and accessibility of the pipes. We minimize disruption and restore water service as quickly as possible.
